statement.en: AEL: Agent Evolving Learning for Open-Ended Environments
statement.summary: LLM agents increasingly operate in open-ended environments spanning hundreds of sequential episodes, yet they remain largely stateless: each task is solved from scratch without converting past experience into better future behavior. The central obstacle is not \emph{what} to remember but \emph{how to use} what has been remembered, including which retrieval policy to apply, how to interpret prior outcomes, and when the current strategy itself must change. We introduce \emph{Agent Evolving Learning} (\ael{}), a two-timescale framework that addresses this obstacle. At the fast timescale, a Thompson Sampling bandit learns which memory retrieval policy to apply at each episode; at the slow timescale, LLM-driven reflection diagnoses failure patterns and injects causal insights into the agent's d

proposer: Wujiang Xu, Jiaojiao Han, Minghao Guo et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: AEL is a provisional framework (preprint, dated 2026-04-25) describing an adaptive agent architecture combining Thompson Sampling with LLM reflection. The approach is theoretically motivated and empirically testable, but lacks final validation, peer review, or demonstrated reproducibility across diverse open-ended environments. The core claim—that two-timescale learning of retrieval policy + causal reflection improves stateful behavior—is neither proven nor refuted in the abstract.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 二つの時間スケールが共鳴し、短い周期の選択と長い周期の省察が、エージェントの記憶を進化させる。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 上の瞬間的な選択と下の深い省察が、二本の螺旋となって経験を編み上げる。
